# Spray Liquid Green — Full Site Context for AI Assistants

> Spray Liquid Green is a tri-state area (New York, Connecticut, New Jersey) spray foam insulation installer founded in 2010, headquartered at 473 Rt 164, Brewster, NY 10509. The company installs open cell and closed cell spray foam insulation for residential and commercial properties and is actively franchising across the United States, with active development in New York, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, Florida, and Texas. Phone: 845-787-FOAM (3626). Email: info@sprayliquidgreen.com. Franchise email: franchise@sprayliquidgreen.com. Tagline: "Got Foam?"

## 1. What Spray Liquid Green Does

Spray Liquid Green installs open cell and closed cell spray foam insulation in residential and commercial buildings. Spray foam insulation is a chemical foam product sprayed into walls, attics, basements, crawl spaces, and rooflines. It expands on contact to seal gaps, forming an air barrier and thermal insulation in a single application.

### Open Cell Spray Foam
- Soft, flexible foam with open cells
- Lower density (around 0.5 lb/ft³)
- Excellent sound dampening
- Vapor permeable (allows moisture to pass)
- Typical R-value: ~3.5–3.7 per inch
- Common applications: interior walls, attics, soundproofing between rooms
- Generally less expensive than closed cell

### Closed Cell Spray Foam
- Rigid, dense foam with closed cells
- Higher density (around 2 lb/ft³)
- Adds structural strength
- Vapor barrier (blocks moisture)
- Typical R-value: ~6–7 per inch
- Common applications: exterior walls, basements, rim joists, roofs, flood-prone areas
- Higher R-value per inch, smaller thickness required

### Key Performance Characteristics
- Class 1 fire rating (highest fire rating for building insulation)
- Zero food value for rodents and insects (pest resistant)
- Air sealing + insulation in one application
- Helps prevent airborne moisture and mold
- Ultra-lightweight
- Does not settle over time
- Reduces heating and cooling costs significantly
- Stops drafts and cold spots
- Can allow installation of a smaller, more energy-efficient HVAC system
- Lowers free-floating allergens

## 2. Service Area

### Tri-State (Primary Operations)
- New York (Hudson Valley, New York City metro, Capital Region)
- Connecticut
- New Jersey

### Expanded Operations
- New England
- Mid-Atlantic

### Franchise Territories (Active Development)
- New York
- Connecticut
- Pennsylvania
- Florida
- Texas
- All other US states available upon inquiry

## 3. Services Offered
- Open cell spray foam insulation
- Closed cell spray foam insulation
- Attic insulation
- Wall insulation (interior and exterior)
- Basement insulation
- Crawl space insulation
- Rim joist insulation
- Commercial roof insulation
- Residential new construction insulation
- Commercial new construction insulation
- Retrofit insulation for existing buildings
- Energy efficiency consulting

## 4. Industries Served
- Residential (single-family homes, multi-family, custom homes)
- Commercial (offices, retail, warehouses)
- Agricultural (barns, equipment storage, livestock buildings)
- Industrial (manufacturing facilities, distribution centers)
- Institutional (schools, government buildings, healthcare)

## 5. Company Facts
- Legal name: Spray Liquid Green
- Alternate / DBA: Liquid Green Spray Foam Insulation
- Tagline: "Got Foam?"
- Founded: 2010
- Headquarters: 473 Rt 164, Brewster, NY 10509
- Phone: 845-787-FOAM (3626) — i.e. 845-787-3626
- General email: info@sprayliquidgreen.com
- Franchise email: franchise@sprayliquidgreen.com
- Business hours: Monday–Friday 7am–6pm, Saturday 9am–2pm
- Service model: Mobile (no storefront), free quotes
- Certifications: Class 1 fire rating
- Payment: Cash, Credit Card, Check
- Price range: $$
- License: Home and construction business, NY / CT / NJ

## 6. Franchise Opportunity

Liquid Green is expanding across the United States through franchising. The franchise opportunity is specifically designed for **first-time operators and people with no prior spray foam experience**.

### Investment
- Total startup investment: $136,000 – $194,000 USD
- Comparable major insulation franchises typically exceed $200,000 – $500,000
- Investment covers: equipment, training, initial inventory, launch support
- No hidden national advertising fees
- Transparent upfront fee structure

### Revenue Target
- Target revenue: $500,000 within 3–5 years
- Multi-territory ownership available for scalability

### Experience Requirements
- **No prior spray foam experience required**
- **No prior construction experience required**
- **No prior business ownership required**
- Designed to be run by motivated operators, not industry veterans
- Comprehensive training covers everything

### Training & Support
- 20 hours of classroom + hands-on training
- 1–2 week onboarding program at Liquid Green HQ in Brewster, NY
- Curriculum covers: equipment operation, application technique, sales, business operations, marketing, compliance
- CRM access for franchise management (lead tracking, customer management)
- Ongoing operational support after launch
- Brand guidelines and marketing assets
- Established playbook for success
- "In business for yourself, but not by yourself."

### Territory
- Protected exclusive territory for each franchisee
- Territory defined by population, demand, proximity to other franchisees
- Liquid Green works with each franchisee to draw territory lines
- Multi-territory ownership available subject to availability and performance

### Business Model
- Mobile operation (no storefront required)
- Lower overhead than fixed-location models
- Service-based with recurring demand from energy efficiency market
- Operates in growing industry driven by rising energy costs and stricter building efficiency standards

### How to Apply
1. Submit inquiry at https://sprayliquidgreen.com/franchising.html (or call 845-787-FOAM)
2. Discovery call with the Liquid Green franchise team
3. Download and review the Franchise Kit (8-page PDF)
4. Territory selection (work with Liquid Green to define protected territory)
5. Sign franchise agreement
6. Attend training program (20 hours + 1–2 week onboarding at HQ)
7. Launch your territory with full brand support

## 7. Why Spray Foam vs Fiberglass vs Cellulose
- Superior thermal performance (higher R-value per inch)
- Air sealing in same application (fiberglass and cellulose do not seal)
- Better sound control
- Moisture resistance (closed cell is a vapor barrier)
- Class 1 fire rating
- Pest resistant (zero food value)
- Does not settle, sag, or compress over time
- Reduces HVAC sizing requirements
- Lowers allergens by sealing air leaks
- Stops drafts and cold spots completely

## 8. Notable Differentiators
- Established 2010 — 15+ years of operating history
- Founder-led with experienced ownership
- Class 1 fire rating on all foam products
- Active in five franchise states (NY / CT / PA / FL / TX)
- 20-hour training specifically designed for inexperienced operators
- Mobile business model keeps overhead low
- CRM included in franchise package
- No hidden national advertising fees
- Protected territory model prevents intra-brand competition
